supersedes: Run 3 (same day, mid-SF2 snapshot). Run 3's exact findings.md/snapshot.json are preserved in git at commit 399ec52; findings.md in this folder keeps the full Run 3 text with the Run 4 section appended.

ARG.optaNote: same staleness caveat; with one match left, title odds = final-match odds

ENG.optaNote: resolved to 0 by elimination Jul 15 (lost SF2 1-2 to Argentina)

sf2_ENG_v_ARG.source: Opta match preview Jul 15; 90-min split ENG 37.3 / draw 30.7 / ARG 32.0

outcomes.note: Both semifinals resolved. SF2: England 1-2 Argentina (Jul 15, Atlanta; Gordon 55'; E. Fernandez 85', L. Martinez 90+2' from a Messi cross; ARG 64% possession, xG ~1.84 v ~0.53; referee Ismail Elfath, USA - no officiating controversy of substance). Final: Spain v Argentina, Jul 19, MetLife. Grading of Run 1-3 scorecards belongs to the score step.

carriedForwardFromRun3: All Run 1-3 verified facts stand: Spain 2-0 France with the disputed Digne-on-Yamal penalty (Deschamps/Collina/Hackett), market flip to Spain, Opta Jul 12 figures partially resolved by reality, Silver paywall note, penalty superlative softening, five named banks, Miami Herald ~$90M, La Nacion $260M/$57M attribution, Tofoni ~3h videoconference interview (witness, not charged), Tapia travel-authorization nugget, AFA pushback quote, no US charges, FBI declined comment.
newSinceRun3: England 1-2 Argentina (Jul 15, Atlanta): Gordon 55'; Enzo Fernandez 85' from distance, Lautaro Martinez 90+2' header from a Messi cross. ARG dominated (64% possession, xG ~1.84 v ~0.53 per ESPN). First Argentina knockout win of the run inside 90 minutes. Final set: Spain v Argentina, Jul 19, MetLife, 3 p.m. ET.

The pre-match officiating storm fizzled: US referee Ismail Elfath was dubbed 'Messi's lucky charm' (2022-final fourth official, viral 4-0 record around Messi teams) and led the first all-US crew in a WC semifinal - then the match produced no disputed call worth a headline (Gordon's goal stood after a routine offside check). Added as timeline node england-argentina-semi + H4 forPoint: under the brightest lights so far, the pattern regressed.

Opta's SF2 preview (Jul 15) leaned England (52.3% to advance; 90-min ENG 37.3 / draw 30.7 / ARG 32.0) - the 47.7% branch happened. Both Opta slim semifinal favorites lost (57.7%, 52.3%); added as a calibration postscript to the whowins-favorite-lost section.

Final opening prices (Jul 15 evening): Caesars ESP -170 / ARG +135, bet365 -175/+125, theScore -160/+130, Kalshi 58c/43c, BetMGM 90-min ESP +125/draw +200/ARG +250. BOOKMAKERS refreshed; author ranges re-synthesized to ESP 0.55-0.60 / ARG 0.40-0.45.

No suspensions for the final on either side (yellows wiped post-QF, no reds in the semis); Spain has one extra rest day (semi Jul 14 v Jul 15) - added to the fatigue section. Messi: 8 goals (tied lead), 6th World Cup, first competitive international meeting with Lamine Yamal.

Financial thread: no material movement (re-searched); Daily Star's ~19bn pesos (~$13M) domestic-case figure consistent with the published ~$12.7M. Key date now four days out: Tapia must return to Argentina after the Jul 19 final under his travel bond.
Hypothesis bands unchanged (H1 2-8, H2 70-90/45-65, H3 35-55, H4 30-50); H4 gains its second semifinal forPoint.
dataChangesThisRun: bracket.ts unchanged (SF2 result was already filled and ESPN-verified at 14:10 local; ESP 2045 v ARG 2000 already yields ~56/44 at nudge=0 from the final-set state).

models.ts: BOOKMAKERS refreshed to post-SF2 final prices (Caesars -170/.630, +135/.426; comment lists bet365/theScore/Kalshi and the BetMGM 90-min line); AUTHOR_RANGES -> ESP .55-.60 / ARG .40-.45; OPTA comment extended with the two resolved semifinal branches (57.7%, 52.3%).
hypotheses.json h4: new forPoint (SF2 clean under maximum scrutiny) + espn-eng-arg, aljazeera-elfath sources.

sections.json: whowins-intro (market now fresh, Opta-only caveat, 55-60/40-45 spoiler); whowins-favorite-lost (calibration postscript: both slim favorites lost); whowins-fatigue (streak broken in 90, Spain's extra rest day).
